content.full_text:
TOWN OF COLMA • COLMA TOWN HALL
1198 EL CAMINO RD

Colma, California 94014 • 415-997-8300

REPLY TO:     Robert H. Cornell, City Attorney
              525 Market Street, Suite 3700
              San Francisco, CA 94105-2745
              Tel: 415/495-8800
              Fax: 415/495-4837

                                          April 1, 1991




State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Ladies/Gentlemen:

     Request is made for advice on behalf of Dennis Fisicaro and
Ray Boudewyn, who are incumbent City Councilmen of the Town of
Colma and are also volunteer firemen of the Colma Fire Protection
District.

     Request is made for an opinion as to whether the above-named
Councilmen are precluded from participating in decisions concern-
ing the Colma Fire Protection District and fire protection provi-
sions for the Town of Colma.

FACTS:

     Both Messrs. Fisicaro and Boudewyn are elected City Council-
men of the Town of Colma. The Colma Fire Protection District is a
separate entity, which encompasses unincorporated Daly City (and an unincorporated part of San Mateo County), and the Town of
Colma. The Fire District is controlled by an elected commission
and is named by volunteers. The department employs an Assistant
Fire Chief/Fire Marshall on an hourly basis. The fire-fighter
volunteers are not paid a salary but receive compensation for each
actual call. This compensation for a volunteer might be as high
as $2,000 per year. There are some additional minor benefits,
such as dinners at the fire house.

     The City Council of the Town of Colma has no direct control
over the operation of the Fire District, but does make the deter-
mination as to whether or not the Town of Colma will stay within
the Fire District, has made cash contributions to the District,
and has entered into joint arrangements with the District with
respect to the acquisition of certain equipment.

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 01, 1991
Page 2

     All of the Fire District that is not within the city limits
of the Town of Colma is within the LAFCo Sphere of Influence of
neighboring Daly City. As portions of the unincorporated area are
annexed to Daly City questions will arise concerning the financial
viability of the Fire District. Decisions will be faced by the
city Council of the Town of Colma concerning contribution of additional funds to the Fire District; remaining in the Fire District;
continuing the Fire District as a City department; establishing a
different city fire department; contracting for outside fire protection services; and whether volunteer staff should be retained.

     We are advised that neither of the above-named Councilmen as
volunteer firemen holds a supervisory position as chief, officer,
or commissioner of the Fire District.

ISSUES:

     Are the above-named Councilmen precluded by virtue of their
position as volunteer firemen with the Colma Fire Protection
District from participating in City decisions with respect to
contributions to or relationships with the Colma Fire Protection
District or any successor thereto, or in the event the Town of
Colma establishes its own fire department whether or not the
department will continue to use volunteer firemen.

     If you need any further information whatsoever, please do not
hesitate to contact the undersigned.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert H. Cornell
                                        City Attorney


14161.003

1.90,212

MYERS, WIDDERS & GIBSON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

J. ROGER MYERS
MONTE L. WIDDERS
KELTON LEE GIBSON
ROBERT I. LONG
JEFFREY E. SACHSE

8425 EVERGLADES STREET, SUITE 100
VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93003
(805) 644-7168
(805) 656-5740

PLEASE REPLY TO
POST OFFICE BOX 7809
VENTURA, CALIFORNIA 93006

TELECOPIER: (805) 644-7390

June 17, 1991

Jill Stecher, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Staal, Gardner & Dunne, Inc.--
Request for Informal Assistance

Dear Ms. Stecher:

In accordance with our telephone conversation of May 30, 1991,
enclosed please find copies of the contracts between Staal, Gardner
& Dunne, Inc. and the City of Simi Valley concerning engineering
consulting services services provided to Simi Valley by Staal,
Gardner. I would appreciate it if you would now respond to my
letter to the Commission, dated March 12, 1990, a copy of which I
enclose for your convenience.

If you should have any questions or require any further
information, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

MYERS, WIDDERS & GIBSON

By

Monte L. Widders

MLW:mem
Enclosures

sections.facts:
Both Messrs. Fisicaro and Boudewyn are elected City Councilmen of the Town of Colma. The Colma Fire Protection District is a separate entity, which encompasses unincorporated Colma, Broadmoor (an unincorporated part of San Mateo County), and the Town of Colma. The Fire District is controlled by an elected commission and is manned by volunteers. The department employs an Assistant Fire Chief/Fire Marshall on an hourly basis. The fire-fighter volunteers are not paid a salary but receive compensation for each actual call. This compensation for a volunteer might be as high as $2,000 per year. There are some additional minor benefits, such as dinners at the fire house.

The City Council of the Town of Colma has no direct control over the operation of the Fire District, but does make the determination as to whether or not the Town of Colma will stay within the Fire District, has made cash contributions to the District, and has entered into joint arrangements with the District with respect to the acquisition of certain equipment.

Fair Political Practices Commission  
April 01, 1991  

All of the Fire District that is not within the city limits of the Town of Colma is within the LAFCo Sphere of Influence of neighboring Daly City. As portions of the unincorporated area are annexed to Daly City questions will arise concerning the financial viability of the Fire District. Decisions will be faced by the City Council of the Town of Colma concerning contribution of additional funds to the Fire District; remaining in the Fire District; continuing the Fire District as a City department; establishing a different City fire department; contracting for outside fire protection services; and whether volunteer staff should be retained.

We are advised that neither of the above-named Councilmen as volunteer firemen holds a supervisory position as chief, officer, or commissioner of the Fire District.

ISSUES:

Are the above-named Councilmen precluded by virtue of their position as volunteer firemen with the Colma Fire Protection District from participating in City decisions with respect to contributions to or relationships with the Colma Fire Protection District or any successor thereto, or in the event the Town of Colma establishes its own fire department whether or not the department will continue to use volunteer firemen.

If you need any further information whatsoever, please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned.
